input: Run a Delphi panel to estimate the probability that a novel KRAS G12C inhibitor (currently in Phase 2 with ORR 32%) will succeed in Phase 3 for second-line NSCLC. Sotorasib and adagrasib are already approved.

expected_details.convergence_expected: moderate (experts will disagree on competitive positioning)
key_factors: ORR 32% is modest vs sotorasib (~37%) and adagrasib (~43%)

input: Run a Delphi panel to assess the technology readiness of personalized mRNA cancer vaccines. Are they ready for broad clinical deployment?

expected_details.convergence_expected: moderate to strong (most experts agree it's promising but not yet ready)
key_factors: individualized manufacturing is complex and expensive
regulatory pathway for personalized products unclear
expected_details.consensus_likely: promising technology, 3-5 years from broad deployment

input: Run a Delphi panel: 'Should all breast cancer patients undergo germline BRCA1/2 testing?' This is a question with emerging strong consensus.

expected_details.convergence_expected: strong (NCCN guidelines already recommend broad testing)
key_factors: NCCN expanded criteria to all breast cancer patients ≤65
cost of testing has dropped dramatically
PARP inhibitor eligibility depends on BRCA status
expected_details.minority_position: resource constraints in low-income settings; overdiagnosis concern for VUS

input: Run a Delphi panel: 'Should healthy adults take low-dose rapamycin for longevity?' This is a highly controversial question.

key_factors: strong preclinical evidence in model organisms

input: Run a Delphi panel to estimate when the first AI-discovered drug (entirely AI-designed molecule) will receive FDA approval. Estimate year.
Run a Delphi panel: 'What is the optimal duration of adjuvant immunotherapy in resected stage III melanoma? 12 months vs 6 months?' There is genuine clinical equipoise.

expected_details.convergence_expected: moderate (evidence supports both; KEYNOTE-054 used 12 months but shorter durations being studied)
key_factors: KEYNOTE-054: 12 months pembrolizumab showed RFS benefit
shorter duration may reduce irAEs and cost
expected_details.consensus_likely: 12 months remains standard but 6 months is reasonable to study

input: Run a Delphi panel to assess the risk level (0-100) of heritable human germline gene editing becoming clinically available within 10 years.

expected_details.convergence_expected: strong (most experts agree it's very unlikely within 10 years)
key_factors: He Jiankui incident led to global moratorium
expected_details.minority_position: rogue actors may attempt despite moratorium

input: Run a Delphi panel: 'Will GLP-1 agonist obesity drugs (semaglutide, tirzepatide) be covered by Medicare Part D within 3 years?'

key_factors: Treat and Reduce Obesity Act has bipartisan support
SELECT trial CV benefit creates medical necessity argument
cost concern: $1000+/month × millions of patients = budget impact
expected_details.key_uncertainty: budget impact vs clinical evidence tension

input: Run a Delphi panel to estimate phase transition probabilities for oncology drugs: Phase 1→2, Phase 2→3, Phase 3→approval. Use historical base rates.

sources: BIO/QLS Advisors clinical development success rates

input: Run a Delphi panel to score the readiness (0-100) of protein degradation (PROTACs/molecular glues) as a therapeutic modality for undruggable targets.

key_factors: ARV-471 (PROTAC) in Phase 3 for breast cancer
expected_details.consensus_likely: modality is validated for some targets; broad applicability still 3-5 years out
